dialogue.utterance: [UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER], respond to the address redacted in [CITY] for a possible suicide attempt-psychiatric call.
Hi, I'm Jake, one of the EMTs on scene. What led you to call 911 for help today?
She attempted to hang herself in the bathroom with a cord about five minutes ago.
Ma'am, I need to ask you a question. What led to considering this act today?
I just... I felt overwhelmed and completely alone. I didn't know how else to cope with it.
Maria, can you tell me your name, the time we're at, and what occurred?
I'm Maria... I think it's Wednesday or Friday. I was in the bathroom.
Nice, you're making eye contact with me. And what city are you in right now?
We're in [CITY]... or maybe it's a dream?
That's okay. Just tell me your surname, Maria.
My last name is - I don't remember right now.
Maria, can you squeeze my fingers just once for me?
Calms her breath and compresses finger momentarily* Yes, feel that?
Are you able to breathe easily, or is it difficult for you right now?
It's tight around my neck, but I can still breathe. Just feels uncomfortable.
Maria, are you breathing normally or does it require effort?
It's challenging. Like I have to push it out, but I can manage each breath.
Maria, do you feel this thumb against your arm?
Yes, it's a bit sore there now.
Circulation appears normal with no bleeding noted.
Pulse is 106-elevated but stable.
Respiratory rate is 61-very rapid, but not immediately concerning.
Blood pressure is currently 183/73.
SpO2 is 99%-oxygen levels okay.
Blood sugar is 131-within acceptable range.
EKG shows a normal sinus rhythm. No acute cardiac concerns seen.
Maria, can you tell me your date of birth?
Her birthday is July 11, 2001.
Maria, have you been experiencing any unusual symptoms before this happened?
I've been really alone, and I've had these thoughts about not wanting to be alive anymore.
She told me she has a history of depression and she's six months pregnant at [COMPANY_NAME]. Gravida 2, Para 1, abortions 0.
Are you taking any medications lately?
Just my prenatal vitamins.
Can you tell us what happened just before the attempt today?
I found her like that. She grabbed a cord from the hair curler and wrapped it around her neck four times.
Maria, do you have a pain rating you'd use to describe how you feel right now?
No real pain, just a constant overwhelm.
Let me check your pupils and eyes now. Can you look straight at the light?
No signs of bleeding in the throat. Minor swelling is noted on the back of the neck. No JVD or tracheal deviation. HEENT appears atraumatic.
Your skin is pink, warm, and dry with normal turgor. Looks good.
Did you injure anything when this happened?
There's some redness at the back from the cord, but no major cuts.
Maria, I need to check your neurological status. Please squeeze again.
You're alert, but somewhat lethargic.
Maria, could you take a deep breath in for me?
Bilateral breath sounds are clear and equal. Chest is symmetric and atraumatic. No stridor noted.
Pulse is now 84-dropped from initial.
Respiratory rate is 24-much improved. Previously very tachypneic.
Maria, we can adjust your body position to something that feels more comfortable. Okay?
Maria, we're about to lift you gently to the stretcher. We'll do this with a two-person carry for your safety.
4-Lead ECG monitoring has been initiated for ongoing monitoring and observation.
Blood pressure is now 89/64, possibly due to artifact in the second reading.
SpO2 remains stable at 98%.
Blood sugar is not registering. We may need to recheck later.
EKG shows continued sinus rhythm with no new findings.
Consulting with command for the general behavioral restraint protocol, specifically Protocol 3-4.
Protocol confirmed and in use for this patient.
An IV has been established in your left arm with an 18-gauge catheter.
Maria, where do you want to be taken for treatment?
[COMPANY_NAME] is where I'd prefer to go.
Maria, I will now help to secure you onto the stretcher. Ready?
You are secured on the stretcher. We'll move you to the ambulance safely now.
We are treating a 19-year-old woman, reporting a suicide attempt using a power cord about five minutes ago. She is alert and oriented x4 with a GCS of 15.
Patient has a patent airway with some breathing discomfort, and elevated vitals on initial assessment.
An IV has been placed in the left arm, EKG remains in sinus rhythm with no acute findings.
She is six months pregnant-gravida 2, para 1, no previous abortions. No significant pain reported. Glucose was 0 in the second reading.
Patient has a history of major depression. No known drug allergies and no significant current medications. Care will be transferred to the receiving facility at [COMPANY_NAME].
Patient is en route to [COMPANY_NAME]. Arrival expected within five minutes.
On-scene transport occurred without incident. She was transferred to the stretcher and then into the ambulance.
She was then moved on-scene into the hospital on arrival and transferred to the hospital bed using a sheet pull.
All movements were performed safely with no complications noted.
Patient reported zero pain at time of assessment and was breathing stably throughout the encounter.
